tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Nine-Year-Old Oliver Scores His First Rugby Try, Embraced by Opposing Players' Grace
stories.summary: Nine-year-old Oliver, battling a rare genetic disease, scores his first rugby try while his dad pushes his wheelchair and opposing players gracefully fake tackles.

stories.headline: Lost Voice Restored: ALS Patient Speaks for Hours Again Through Brain-Computer Link
stories.summary: Casey Harrell, paralyzed by ALS, now speaks fluently for twelve hours straight using a BrainGate implant that restores his own pre-illness voice.

stories.headline: 40-Year-Old Cape Verde Goalkeeper Becomes World Cup Hero, Brings Mother's Dream to Life
stories.summary: Goalkeeper Vozinha, 40, becomes a viral sensation stopping Spain's nearly 30 shots to lead 67th-ranked Cabo Verde to an upset at the World Cup.

stories.headline: Mountain Stranger Gives Away 2,200 Custom Hats—One Hiker's Act of Joy
stories.summary: Curtis Jones spent his entire $72,000 retirement bonus on custom hats, gifting over 2,200 to hikers around Boulder, Colorado—each worth about $50.

stories.headline: Japan's Golden Eagle, Born in Tokyo Zoo, Takes Flight in the Wild
stories.summary: A young endangered eagle bred in captivity soars free in Miyagi, marking Japan's first successful zoo-to-wild reintegration of this national treasure.

stories.headline: Sanitation Workers Rescue Malnourished Dog from Garbage Bin, Adopt Him on the Spot
stories.summary: Milwaukee sanitation workers Naz Nalls and Omar pull a starving 10-pound dog from a garbage bin that miraculously fell off their truck, and supervisor Halverson adopts him on the spot.

stories.headline: AI Cracks 18 Rare Diseases That Stumped the World's Best Specialists
stories.summary: OpenAI's o3 model diagnosed 18 previously unsolved rare genetic cases at Boston Children's Hospital, finally connecting desperate families to answers after years of searching.

stories.headline: Injured Pitcher Returns for State Final, Carries Kaukauna to First Title in 73 Years
stories.summary: Senior pitcher Paxton Schuh, given only a 2% chance after meniscus surgery, returns to lead Kaukauna to its first state baseball title in 73 years.

stories.headline: Young Supermarket Worker Becomes Guardian Angel for Struggling Elderly Customer
stories.summary: Phil Snow-Griffiths, a 23-year-old Asda produce team leader, arranges a taxi and loads groceries for an elderly customer struggling alone at checkout.

stories.headline: Golfer's Stunning Two-Hour Turnaround Rewrites U.S. Open History
stories.summary: Keith Mitchell becomes golf history's only player to post both a catastrophic 41 and an incredible 29 in a single U.S. Open round.

stories.headline: Arabian Oryx Thrives Again in Saudi Reserve After 90 Years of Absence
stories.summary: Endangered Arabian oryx breed successfully in King Salman Royal Reserve, with newborns already flourishing across multiple protected areas—a species brought back from the brink.

stories.headline: 21-Year-Old Filipino Wildcard Stuns Former Wimbledon Champion at Berlin Open
stories.summary: Alex Eala defeats world No. 2 Elena Rybakina 7-5, 6-4, scoring one of the biggest victories of her young career on tennis's grandest stages.

stories.headline: Marsh Deer Escapes Critical Danger as Argentina's Wetlands Come Alive
stories.summary: Argentina's marsh deer rebounds from critically endangered to nearly threatened status, offering hope for the country's wetland ecosystems.

stories.headline: World's First 100% Hydrogen Ship Engine Certified—Emitting Only Water and Clean Air
stories.summary: Lloyd's Register approves a marine engine running entirely on hydrogen, eliminating fossil fuels and proving zero-emission shipping is no longer a dream.

stories.headline: Gharial Revival: 160 Hatchlings Born as Ancient Reptile Returns to Indian Rivers
stories.summary: India's Nandankanan Zoo celebrates natural gharial breeding after 40 years, with over 160 hatchlings thriving in protected zones since 2021.
